sprint: World Builder — Duplicate Silently Failing on Large Worlds
task: Follow-up bug found after the overflow-menu Duplicate fix: a user report 'nothing happens, no error, no new card, the page just flickers' described a symptom the id-regeneration fix couldn't explain. Reproduced directly with a real oversized test Project: js/projectStore.js's duplicate() deep-copies a Project's entire files map (every uploaded Thumbnail/Hero Image/asset) but -- unlike save(), which AV-009 already hardened -- discarded _writeAll()'s result and always returned the in-memory copy regardless of whether the write actually reached localStorage. Duplicating roughly doubles a World's own storage footprint, which is exactly the realistic case that pushes an image-heavy World over this browser's per-origin quota. Fixed by making duplicate() mirror save()'s {project, ok, error} shape; the shared _duplicateProject() helper now propagates that result and shows a real, honest alert on failure instead of silently doing nothing. Verified via Playwright against a real, reproducible browser-level quota failure: before, silent failure with zero signal; after, the alert fires and no phantom project is created. Normal-size Worlds unaffected. goldenBuild.js's full 30-assertion suite passes unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
